sections.paragraph:
m. Eze 41:15 (קרי), 16 Eze 41:16, 42:3 , Eze 42:5, a kind of columns [see below]; (see especially Eze 42:5, comp. verse Eze 42:6). LXX. and Vulg. chap. 42 , render it περίστυλον, porticus. Root אָתַק.

[“A term in architecture, signifying a decrement; where a story or portico is drawn in, an offset, ledge, terrace. It is a verbal Hiph. from נָתַק to tear away, cut off. So Böttcher recently (Proben, page 350); but so too Abulwalid long before, i.q. نصيل segment, increment. See his words quoted in Thes. Append. s. h. v.” Ges. add.]
